question:
Among the following, the number of tranquilizer/s is/are

A. Chloroliazepoxide
B. Veronal
C. Valium
D. Salvarsan
solution:
A drug that calms and soothes, and reduces stress and tension is called tranquilizer. Tranquilizers are used to treat anxiety and insomnia.

Chlorodiazepoxide, Veronal, Valium are tranquilizers, where as salvarsan is antibiotic.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements: One is labelled as Assertion A and the other labelled as Reason R.
Assertion A: Antihistamines do not affect the secretion of acid in stomach.
Reason R : Antiallergic and antacid drugs work on different receptors.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements: One is labelled as Assertion A and the other labelled as Reason R.
Assertion A: Antihistamines do not affect the secretion of acid in stomach.
Reason R : Antiallergic and antacid drugs work on different receptors.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is false but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is true

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are true and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is false

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is not the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>.
solution:
Antihistamines do not affect the secretion of acid in stomach, the reason is that antiallergic and antacid drugs work on different receptors.

So, both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Thyroxine, the hormone has given below structure

The percentage of iodine in thyroxine is ____ $\%$. (nearest integer)
(Given molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \mathrm{C}: 12. \mathrm{H}: 1, \mathrm{O}: 16$, $\mathrm{N}: 14, \mathrm{I}: 127)$
question:
Thyroxine, the hormone has given below structure

The percentage of iodine in thyroxine is ____ $\%$. (nearest integer)
(Given molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \mathrm{C}: 12. \mathrm{H}: 1, \mathrm{O}: 16$, $\mathrm{N}: 14, \mathrm{I}: 127)$
solution:
$\rightarrow$ Molecular formula of Thyroxine $\Rightarrow$ $\mathrm{C}_{15} \mathrm{H}_{11} \mathrm{O}_4 \mathrm{NI}_4$
$\rightarrow$ Molecular mass of Thyroxine -
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{C} \rightarrow 15 \times 12=180 \\ & \mathrm{H} \rightarrow 11 \times 1=11 \\ & \mathrm{O} \rightarrow 16 \times 4=64 \\ & \mathrm{~N} \rightarrow 14 \times 1=14 \\ & \mathrm{I} \rightarrow 127 \times 4=508\end{aligned}$
$\rightarrow$ Molecular mass of Thyroxine $\Rightarrow 777$
$\begin{aligned}& \rightarrow \% \text { of Iodine }=\frac{508}{777} \times 100 \\& =65.38 \%\end{aligned}$
Nearest integer $=65$

subject: Chemistry
q: With respect to drug-enzyme interaction, identify the wrong statement:
question: With respect to drug-enzyme interaction, identify the wrong statement:
options: Non-Competitive inhibitor binds to the allosteric site
Allosteric inhibitor changes the enzyme's active site
Allosteric inhibitor competes with the enzyme's active site
Competitive inhibitor binds to the enzyme's active site
solution:
Some drugs do not bind to the Enzyme's active site. These bind to a different site of enzyme which called allosteric site.

This binding of inhibitor at allosteric site changes the shape of the active site in such a way that substrate can not recognize it. Such inhibitor is known as Non-competitive inhibitor.

q:
The correct match between column-I and column-II is
 
Column-I (drug) Column-II (test)
(A) Chloroxylenol (P) Carbylamine test
(B) Norethindrone (Q) Sodium hydrogen carbonate test
(C) Sulphapyridine (R) Ferric chloride test
(D) Penicillin (S) Baeyer’s test
question:
The correct match between column-I and column-II is
 
Column-I (drug) Column-II (test)
(A) Chloroxylenol (P) Carbylamine test
(B) Norethindrone (Q) Sodium hydrogen carbonate test
(C) Sulphapyridine (R) Ferric chloride test
(D) Penicillin (S) Baeyer’s test
solution:
Chloroxylenol is a phenol derivative and hence, can be identified with FeCl3 test.

The primary amine of sulphapyridine can be identified with carbylamine test.

The aliphatic alkyne of Norethindrone can be identified by the Baeyer's reagent.

Penicillin gives test with sodium hydrogen carbonate.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List-I with List-II

  List-I   List-II
(A) Antipyretic (I) Reduces pain
(B) Analgesic (II) Reduces stress
(C) Tranquilizer (III) Reduces fever
(D) Antacid (IV) Reduces acidity(stomach)

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Match List-I with List-II

  List-I   List-II
(A) Antipyretic (I) Reduces pain
(B) Analgesic (II) Reduces stress
(C) Tranquilizer (III) Reduces fever
(D) Antacid (IV) Reduces acidity(stomach)

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
Analgesics reduce or abolish pain without causing impairment of consciousness, mental confusion, incoordination, paralysis or some other disturbances of nervous system.

Tranquillizers are a class of chemical compounds used for the treatment of stress, and mild or even severe mental diseases. These relieve anxiety, stress, irritability or excitement by inducing a sense of well-being. They form an essential component of sleeping pills.

Treatment for acidity was administration of antacids, such as sodium hydrogen carbonate or a mixture of aluminium and magnesium hydroxide. However, excessive hydrogen carbonate can make the stomach alkaline and trigger the production of even more acid.

An antipyretic is a substance that reduces fever. Antipyretics cause the hypothalamus to override a prostaglandin-induced increase in temperature.

Hence,

(A) Antipyretic Reduces fever
(B) Analgesic Reduces pain
(C) Tranquilizer Reduces stress
(D) Antacid Reduces acidity (Stomach)

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following compounds is an example of hypnotic drug?
question: Which of the following compounds is an example of hypnotic drug?
solution:
Derivatives of barbituric acid viz., veronal, amytal, nembutal, luminal and seconal constitute an important class of tranquilizers. These derivatives are called barbiturates. Barbiturates are hypnotic. i.e., sleep producing agents.

Amytal is hypnotic drug used to treat sleep disorder.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Statements about Enzyme Inhibitor Drugs are given below :

(A) There are Competitive and Non-competitive inhibitor drugs.

(B) These can bind at the active sites and allosteric sites.

(C) Competitive Drugs are allosteric site blocking drugs.

(D) Non-competitive Drugs are active site blocking drugs.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Statements about Enzyme Inhibitor Drugs are given below :

(A) There are Competitive and Non-competitive inhibitor drugs.

(B) These can bind at the active sites and allosteric sites.

(C) Competitive Drugs are allosteric site blocking drugs.

(D) Non-competitive Drugs are active site blocking drugs.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution: Enzyme inhibitors are molecules that interact with enzymes (temporary or permanent) in some way and reduce the rate of an enzyme-catalyzed reaction or prevent enzymes to work in a normal manner. Enzyme inhibitors can be competitive inhibitors (inhibit the attachment of substrate on active site of enzyme) and non-competitive inhibitor (changes the active site of enzyme after binding at allosteric site.) So, statement A and B are correct. But statement C and D are incorrect.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following enzyme converts starch into maltose?
question: Which of the following enzyme converts starch into maltose?
solution:
Maltose is obtained by partial hydrolysis of starch by the enzyme diastase present in Malt.
$2\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{10} \mathrm{O}_5\right)_n+n \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \underset{n \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O}_6 \text { (Maltose) }}{\stackrel{\text { Diastase }}{\longrightarrow}}$

q: Stearic acid and polyethylene glycol react to form which one of the following soap/s detergents?
question: Stearic acid and polyethylene glycol react to form which one of the following soap/s detergents?
solution:
Non-ionic detergents do not contain any ion in their constitution. One such detergent is formed when stearic acid reacts with polyethylene glycol.

CH3CH216COOHStearic acid+HOCH2CH2OnCH2CH2OHPolyethyleneglycol →-H2OCH3CH216COOCH2CH2OnCH2CH2OH

 A type of detergent in which the active part of the molecule is a positive ion (cation). Cationic detergents are usually quaternary ammonium salts.

Anionic detergent is a synthetic detergent in which a lipophilic hydrocarbon group of the molecule is an anion. A detergent molecule consists of a long hydrocarbon chain and a water-soluble negative ionic group.

question: Some drugs bind to a site other than, the active site of an enzyme. This site is known as
solution:
Some drugs do not bind to the enzyme’s active site. These bind to a different site of enzyme which is called allosteric site. This binding of inhibitor at allosteric site changes the shape of the active site in such a way that substrate cannot recognise it. 

Non-competitive inhibitor changes the active site of enzyme after binding at allosteric site

q: A doctor prescribed the drug Equanil to a patient. The patient was likely to have symptoms of which disease?
question: A doctor prescribed the drug Equanil to a patient. The patient was likely to have symptoms of which disease?
solution:
Tranquilizers are neurologically active drugs used to treat stress and severe or mild mental disorders.
Equanil is an example of a tranquilizer that helps in controlling depression and hypertension.
This medication is used short term to treat symptoms of anxiety and nervousness. It acts on certain centres of the brain to help calm the nervous system.

subject: Chemistry
q: How many of the following drugs is/are example(s) of broad spectrum antibiotic? Ofloxacin, Penicillin <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi></math>, Terpineol, Salvarsan
question: How many of the following drugs is/are example(s) of broad spectrum antibiotic? Ofloxacin, Penicillin <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi></math>, Terpineol, Salvarsan
solution:
The range of bacteria or other microorganisms that are affected by a certain antibiotic is expressed as its spectrum of action. Antibiotics which kill or inhibit a wide range of Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria are said to be broad spectrum antibiotics. Those effective mainly against Gram-positive or Gram-negative bacteria are narrow spectrum antibiotics.

Salvarsan is an antibacterial drug.-As=As-linkage present in arsphenamine or Salvarsan

Penicillin G is a narrow spectrum antibiotic

Ofloxacin is an important broad spectrum antibiotic.

Terpineol along with chloroxylenol are the components of dettol  which is used as antiseptic .

Hence, Ofloxacin is the only broad spectrum antibiotic among the given options.

solution: We know that drugs help us diagnose and cure numerous illnesses. But any drug consumed by humans can actually have two kinds of effects - therapeutic effects and side effects. The goal is to prescribe the ideal drug, the one that only has a therapeutic effect.

q: Among the following organic acids, the acid present in rancid butter is :
question: Among the following organic acids, the acid present in rancid butter is :
solution: Butyric acid is a fatty acid occuring in the form of esters in animal fats when butter goes rancid, butyric acid is liberated from the glyceride by hydrolysis, leading to unpleasant odor.
